title: Rivian Q4 Earnings Highlights - Revenue Beat, EPS Miss, 2024 Guidance
text: Rivian exceeded expectations with $1.32 billion in Q4 revenue, surpassing the Street consensus of $1.26 billion. The company reported a loss of $1.36 per share, which fell short of estimates projecting a $1.32 per share loss. In the fourth quarter, Rivian produced 17,541 vehicles and delivered 13,972. For the full 2023 fiscal year, the company produced 57,232 vehicles and delivered 50,122 vehicles. The company maintained a strong cash position of $9.37 billion in cash and equivalents, with total liquidity of $10.47 billion including a revolving credit facility. Rivian projects 2024 production of 57,000 vehicles and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $2.7 billion, citing historically high interest rates and economic uncertainties as headwinds. The company announced a 10% workforce reduction among salaried employees to improve cost efficiency. Additionally, CEO RJ Scaringe emphasized the company's focus on achieving positive margins and building the go-to-market function. The Rivian R2 was scheduled to be unveiled on March 7.
